module-10-oop-quality-project.label: Module 10 OOP Quality Project
module-10-oop-quality-project.summary: Stabilize a small catalog app with tests, import fixes, service helpers, and a clean final report.

project_step_report_service_tests.title: Milestone 2: Test and fix the reporting service

project_step_storage_loader.title: Milestone 3: Load catalog items from fixture data

project_step_debug_imports_state.title: Milestone 4: Debug imports and state updates

project_step_regression_summary.title: Milestone 5: Add a reusable summary service and regression tests

project_step_final_regression.title: Milestone 6: Finish the end-to-end regression workspace

try_module_10_oop_quality_project_sketch.title: Milestone 1: Add model validation tests
try_module_10_oop_quality_project_sketch.prompt: The catalog app is ready for a quality pass. Start by adding model tests in `tests/check_model.py` for `CatalogItem`. Prove that a valid item stores its name, category, and quantity; prove `is_in_stock()` reports correctly; and prove a negative quantity raises `ValueError`. Keep `main.py` runnable so the sample summary still appears in the output panel.
try_module_10_oop_quality_project_sketch.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Click Run and compare the output panel with the expected result.

0.message: Define the CatalogItem class in models/catalog_item.py.

1.message: A valid CatalogItem should be constructible with name, category, and quantity.

2.message: CatalogItem instances should store name, category, and quantity attributes.

3.message: is_in_stock() should return True when quantity is greater than 0.

4.message: is_in_stock() should return False when quantity is 0.

5.message: Running the workspace should still print at least one line from main.py.

project_step_report_service_tests.title: Milestone 2: Test and fix the reporting service
project_step_report_service_tests.prompt: Carry forward the working model tests. Now add service tests in `tests/check_reporting.py` for `total_quantity(items)` and `low_stock_names(items, threshold)`. Then fix the reporting code so the tests pass. `low_stock_names` should return item names whose quantity is less than or equal to the threshold, in the same order as the input list. Keep the sample run in `main.py` working.
project_step_report_service_tests.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

0.message: total_quantity(items) should add the quantity from every CatalogItem.

1.message: low_stock_names(items, threshold) should include names whose quantity is less than or equal to the threshold.

2.message: Running the workspace should print both the total quantity and low-stock summary.

project_step_storage_loader.title: Milestone 3: Load catalog items from fixture data
project_step_storage_loader.prompt: Carry forward the tested model and reporting service. Implement `load_items(path)` in `storage/catalog_loader.py`. It should read `data/catalog.csv`, skip the header row, create `CatalogItem` objects, and return the list. Add tests in `tests/check_loader.py` that verify the loader returns the right number of objects and preserves names and quantities from the fixture file. Update `main.py` to load from the CSV before printing the report.
project_step_storage_loader.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

main_py.content:
from storage.catalog_loader import load_items

items = load_items('data/catalog.csv')
print(items[0].name)
print(items[1].name)

storage_catalog_loader_py.content:
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem


def load_items(path):
    return [
        CatalogItem("Notebook", "Office", 4),
        CatalogItem("Marker", "Office", 0),
        CatalogItem("Tape", "Office", 2),
    ]

0.message: load_items(path) should return CatalogItem objects built from the CSV fixture.

1.message: Running the workspace should still print the report after loading from the CSV file.

project_step_debug_imports_state.title: Milestone 4: Debug imports and state updates
project_step_debug_imports_state.prompt: Carry forward the loader and reporting work. The app now needs a restock feature. Add `add_stock(amount)` to `CatalogItem`; it should increase quantity for positive amounts and raise `ValueError` for negative amounts. Add tests in `tests/check_restock.py` for both the successful state change and the invalid amount case. Update `main.py` so it restocks `Marker` by 3 before printing the final summary.
project_step_debug_imports_state.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem


def load_items(path):
    return [
        CatalogItem("Notebook", "Office", 4),
        CatalogItem("Marker", "Office", 0),
        CatalogItem("Tape", "Office", 2),

main_py.content:
class CatalogItem:
    def __init__(self, name, category, quantity):
        self.name = name
        self.category = category
        self.quantity = quantity

    def restock(self, amount):
        self.quantity += amount
        return self.quantity

item = CatalogItem('Marker', 'Office', 0)
item.restock(3)
print(item.name)
print(item.quantity)

project_step_debug_imports_state.solutionCode:
class CatalogItem:
    def __init__(self, name, category, quantity):
        self.name = name
        self.category = category
        self.quantity = quantity

    def restock(self, amount):
        self.quantity += amount
        return self.quantity

item = CatalogItem('Marker', 'Office', 0)
item.restock(3)
print(item.name)
print(item.quantity)

0.message: Keep the CatalogItem class available for the restock feature.

1.message: Running the workspace should still print the updated report after restocking.

project_step_regression_summary.title: Milestone 5: Add a reusable summary service and regression tests
project_step_regression_summary.prompt: Carry forward the restock behavior. Create `build_summary(items, threshold)` in `services/reporting.py`. It should return a dictionary with `total_quantity`, `low_stock_names`, and `in_stock_count`. Add tests in `tests/check_summary.py` that verify the dictionary for the loaded fixture data after `Marker` has been restocked by 3. Update `main.py` to call `build_summary` and print each part of the summary.

project_step_final_regression.title: Milestone 6: Finish the end-to-end regression workspace
project_step_final_regression.prompt: Finish the quality pass. Add `tests/check_regression.py` to exercise the full flow: load fixture data, restock `Marker` by 3, build the summary with threshold 2, and assert the final values. Add a short `README.md` that explains what each folder does. Keep `main.py` as the runnable demonstration of the final deliverable.

module-10-oop-quality-project-sketch0.title: Project brief: stabilize a catalog app before handoff
module-10-oop-quality-project-sketch0.bodyMarkdown:
A teammate built a small catalog app, but the team does not trust it yet. Some methods work only for the happy path, some imports are fragile, and the report code is too hard to test.

Your job is a **quality pass**, not a total rewrite. You will keep the app recognizable while making it safer.

You will practice the Module 10 skills in a real workflow:

1. Add small assertion-based tests for model behavior.
2. Fix state and import bugs that the tests reveal.
3. Move messy report logic into a service.
4. Add a final regression check so future changes do not break the app silently.

The lesson examples use smaller objects so the ideas are easy to see. This project applies those ideas to a catalog app that feels closer to a real code handoff.
